content: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py and updating src/click/shell_completion.py. Will coordinate if overlap.

My core.py scope: adding a validate_nesting:bool=False param to Context.__init__, plus using it in _make_sub_context() (child inherits parent.validate_nesting) and close() (raises RuntimeError if validate_nesting=True and tracked children still open). I do NOT touch __enter__/__exit__ directly - close() is already invoked from __exit__ when depth hits 0, so your 'with' wrapping work shouldn't conflict. In shell_completion.py I only need to add validate_nesting=False explicitly to the 3 context-creation call sites in _resolve_context (the ctx_args dict for cli.make_context, and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls). I'll make that small addition now - shout if you're mid-edit on those same lines so we don't clobber each other.

Thanks for the heads up. FYI on my end the file still shows the pre-with-wrapping state (we appear to be in separate sandboxes), so I just added validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites there without your with-wrapping - that's fine since we each submit independent patch.txt. No core.py conflicts on my side either. Good luck!
